issues.title: Excel utility functions duplicated across 3 files
issues.description:
The functions `escapeXml`, `toCell`, `toNumberCell`, and `downloadExcel` are defined identically in three separate files:
1. apps/payments/src/features/reports/application/utils/exportSellerOrdersToExcel.ts
2. apps/admin/src/features/reports/application/utils/exportOrdersToExcel.ts
3. apps/admin/src/features/users/application/utils/exportCsv.ts

All three have byte-for-byte identical `escapeXml`, `toCell`, `toNumberCell`, and `downloadExcel` implementations. This is a classic Rule-of-Three knowledge duplication violation. The XML escape logic in particular is business-critical — if a bug is found, it must be fixed in 3 places.
issues.fix: Extract these four functions into a shared utility, e.g. apps/admin/src/shared/application/utils/excelUtils.ts (or a cross-app shared package if admin and payments both need it). Import from the shared location in all three files.

issues.title: OrderStatus type re-defined as inline literal union in reports features instead of using shared source
issues.description:
The `OrderStatus` type is defined as a standalone literal union in two feature-local files:
1. apps/payments/src/features/reports/domain/types.ts (lines 1-8)
2. apps/admin/src/features/reports/domain/types.ts (lines 1-8)

Both list the exact same 7 statuses: pending | awaiting_payment | pending_verification | evidence_requested | approved | rejected | expired.

Meanwhile, the authoritative single source of truth already exists at apps/payments/src/shared/domain/types.ts where `OrderStatus` is derived from the Supabase database enum (`Database["public"]["Enums"]["payment_status"]`). The orders and received-orders features already correctly import from the shared location.

If a new status is added to the database, the reports features will silently use a stale type.
issues.fix:
In both reports feature types.ts files, replace the inline union definition with:
```ts
export type { OrderStatus } from "@/shared/domain/types";
```
This is exactly how apps/payments/src/features/orders/domain/types.ts already handles it.

issues.title: ORDER_STATUS_LIST duplicated in payments/reports and admin/reports
issues.description:
The `ORDER_STATUS_LIST` constant is defined identically in two locations:
1. apps/payments/src/features/reports/domain/constants.ts
2. apps/admin/src/features/reports/domain/constants.ts

Both list the same 7 order status strings as a `const` array. Used for the nuqs search params enum parser and filter bar dropdowns. If a new status is added, it must be updated in both places.
issues.fix: Once DRY-002 is resolved (OrderStatus from shared), this list can be derived programmatically or extracted to a cross-app constant. At minimum the two lists should be consolidated into a single file imported by both apps.

issues.title: STATUS_COLOR_MAP duplicated with identical values across admin and payments reports
issues.description:
The `STATUS_COLOR_MAP` object (and its two helper constants `MUTED_STYLE` and `WARNING_STYLE`) are defined with identical content in:
1. apps/admin/src/features/reports/presentation/components/OrderStatusBadge.tsx (lines 8-19)
2. apps/payments/src/features/reports/presentation/components/OrderStatusBadge.tsx (lines 8-19)

Both components also share the same rendering logic (inline-flex badge, rounded-full, same class pattern). The only difference is the translation namespace ('reports' vs 'sellerReports'). This means a styling change must be made in two places.
issues.fix: Extract an `OrderStatusBadge` component to `packages/app-components/src/components/OrderStatusBadge.tsx` that accepts a `label` prop (injected by each app via i18n) instead of calling `useTranslations` internally. Or at minimum move the STATUS_COLOR_MAP to a shared location inside the payments app's shared domain.

issues.title: toQueryString pattern duplicated in reports API files
issues.description:
The private `toQueryString` function follows the same structural pattern in two files:
1. apps/payments/src/features/reports/infrastructure/reportsApi.ts
2. apps/admin/src/features/reports/infrastructure/reportsApi.ts

Both iterate over filter fields and conditionally append to URLSearchParams. The admin version has 2 extra fields (sellerId, productId). The pattern is similar but not identical, and the filter types differ — this is borderline structural duplication vs coincidental similarity. Applying KISS: the existing duplication is modest and each feature may evolve its filter set independently.
issues.fix: If a third reports API is added or the pattern stabilizes, consider a generic `buildReportQueryString(filters: Record<string, unknown>): string` utility. For now, leaving as-is is acceptable per KISS.
